title: phaeomeria speciosa
intro: Phaeomeria speciosa, also known as the Hawaiian hibiscus or pua kena, is a species of flowering plant in the mallow family. It is traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to lower blood pressure and reduce inflammation due to its high content of anthocyanins and flavonoids. The health benefits of Phaeomeria speciosa include its potential to improve cardiovascular health, reduce the risk of certain cancers, and alleviate symptoms of menopause. Phaeomeria speciosa is often prepared as a tea or tincture, and it may also be used in combination with other herbs in traditional Hawaiian medicine.
